New Jersey · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Advisory Committee under the New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$120
$120 application and license fee
Renewal
$120
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 8 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Advisory Committee

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 45020, Newark, NJ 07101

Physical Address

124 Halsey St., Newark, NJ 07102

Phone & Fax

Phone: (973) 504-6430

Fax: (973) 648-3355

Email

askconsumeraffairs@dca.lps.state.nj.us

Credential Categories

New Jersey SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

Full credential for independent SLP practice in New Jersey.

Temporary SLP Permit

For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Application Process

How to Apply for a New Jersey Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

From a CAA-accredited program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Test code 5331, score 162.

Complete Clinical Fellowship

36 weeks supervised clinical practice.

Submit Application

Submit to Division of Consumer Affairs with fees.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background checkCriminal background check and fingerprinting.
Telepractice

New Jersey Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

New Jersey permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. New Jersey is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

New Jersey issues a Temporary SLP Permit for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

New Jersey does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full New Jersey licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

New Jersey SLP FAQ

How much does a New Jersey SLP license cost?

$120 initial. $120 biennial renewal.

Is New Jersey part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

No.

Does New Jersey allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes, with a valid NJ license.
